fn process_exchange(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    check_owner(original_account_info, original_token_program.key)?;
    check_owner(original_mint_info, original_token_program.key)?;
    check_owner(new_escrow_info, new_token_program.key)?;
    check_owner(new_account_info, new_token_program.key)?;
    check_owner(new_mint_info, new_token_program.key)?;

    let (token_amount, decimals) = {
        let original_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&original_account_data)?;
        let new_escrow = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&new_escrow_data)?;
        if new_escrow.base.amount < token_amount {
            return Err(ProgramError::InsufficientFunds);
        }
        (original_account.base.amount, original_mint.base.decimals)
    };

    burn_original_tokens(original_token_program.clone(), original_account_info.clone(),
        original_mint_info.clone(), original_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        account_info_iter.as_slice(), token_amount, decimals)?;

    transfer_new_tokens(new_token_program.clone(), new_escrow_info.clone(),
        new_mint_info.clone(), new_account_info.clone(), new_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        &authority_seeds, token_amount, decimals)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
Both burn_original_tokens and transfer_new_tokens use ? for error propagation. Five ownership checks validate all accounts before any CPI. Data borrows are dropped before CPIs to avoid double-borrow.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept 5 check_owner calls (L96-100), burn with ? (L143-151), transfer with ? (L153-162), data borrow/drop pattern (L108-140)

fn process_exchange(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let original_account_data = original_account_info.try_borrow_data()?;
    let original_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&original_account_data)?;
    let token_amount = original_account.base.amount;
    let decimals = original_mint.base.decimals;

    let _ = burn_original_tokens(original_token_program.clone(), original_account_info.clone(),
        original_mint_info.clone(), original_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        account_info_iter.as_slice(), token_amount, decimals);

    transfer_new_tokens(new_token_program.clone(), new_escrow_info.clone(),
        new_mint_info.clone(), new_account_info.clone(), new_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        &authority_seeds, token_amount, decimals)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls
CRITICAL
Burn error is silently discarded with let _ =. If burn fails, new tokens are still transferred — user gets new tokens without burning originals. No ownership checks allow accounts from any program. No escrow balance validation.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - removed 5 check_owner calls (L96-100), replaced burn ? with let _ = (L143-151), removed escrow balance check (L128-134), kept data borrow during CPI (double-borrow risk)

Code:
fn process_propose(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    tokens_to_mint: u64, acceptance_criteria: AcceptanceCriteria,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let (mint_address, mint_bump_seed) = get_mint_address_with_seed(feature_proposal_info.key);
    if mint_address != *mint_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
    }
    let (distributor_token_address, distributor_token_bump_seed) =
        get_distributor_token_address_with_seed(feature_proposal_info.key);
    if distributor_token_address != *distributor_token_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
    }

    invoke(
        &system_instruction::create_account(funder_info.key, feature_proposal_info.key,
            rent.minimum_balance(FeatureProposal::get_packed_len()),
            FeatureProposal::get_packed_len() as u64, program_id),
        &[funder_info.clone(), feature_proposal_info.clone(), system_program_info.clone()],
    )?;

    invoke_signed(
        &system_instruction::create_account(funder_info.key, mint_info.key,
            rent.minimum_balance(Mint::get_packed_len()), Mint::get_packed_len() as u64, &spl_token::id()),
        &[funder_info.clone(), mint_info.clone(), system_program_info.clone()],
        &[mint_signer_seeds],
    )?;

    invoke(
        &spl_token::instruction::initialize_mint(&spl_token::id(), mint_info.key,
            mint_info.key, None, spl_token::native_mint::DECIMALS)?,
        &[mint_info.clone(), spl_token_program_info.clone(), rent_sysvar_info.clone()],
    )?;

    invoke_signed(
        &spl_token::instruction::mint_to(&spl_token::id(), mint_info.key,
            distributor_token_info.key, mint_info.key, &[], tokens_to_mint)?,
        &[mint_info.clone(), distributor_token_info.clone(), spl_token_program_info.clone()],
        &[mint_signer_seeds],
    )?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
All CPIs use ? for error propagation: create_account, initialize_mint, mint_to. PDA addresses verified before use. If any step fails, entire transaction reverts atomically.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept PDA verification (L56-72), invoke/invoke_signed chain all with ? propagation

Code:
fn process_tally(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let feature_proposal_state =
        FeatureProposal::unpack_from_slice(&feature_proposal_info.data.borrow())?;

    match feature_proposal_state {
        FeatureProposal::Pending(acceptance_criteria) => {
            let (acceptance_token_address) = get_acceptance_token_address(feature_proposal_info.key);
            if acceptance_token_address != *acceptance_token_info.key {
                return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
            }
            let (feature_id_address, feature_id_bump_seed) =
                get_feature_id_address_with_seed(feature_proposal_info.key);
            if feature_id_address != *feature_id_info.key {
                return Err(ProgramError::InvalidArgument);
            }

            if clock.unix_timestamp >= acceptance_criteria.deadline {
                FeatureProposal::Expired.pack_into_slice(&mut feature_proposal_info.data.borrow_mut());
                return Ok(());
            }

            let acceptance_token = spl_token::state::Account::unpack(&acceptance_token_info.data.borrow())?;
            if acceptance_token.amount < acceptance_criteria.tokens_required {
                return Ok(());
            }

            invoke_signed(
                &system_instruction::assign(feature_id_info.key, &feature::id()),
                &[feature_id_info.clone(), system_program_info.clone()],
                &[feature_id_signer_seeds],
            )?;

            FeatureProposal::Accepted { tokens_upon_acceptance: acceptance_token.amount }
                .pack_into_slice(&mut feature_proposal_info.data.borrow_mut());
        }
        _ => return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData),
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
invoke_signed with ? for feature assignment. PDA addresses re-derived and verified. Deadline and threshold checked before any CPI. State correctly transitions to Accepted only after successful assignment.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept PDA re-derivation (L238-251), deadline check (L258-262), threshold check (L268-271), invoke_signed with ? (L273-277), state transition (L280-282)

RecordInstruction::CloseAccount => {
    let data_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let destination_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let raw_data = &mut data_info.data.borrow_mut();
    let account_data = bytemuck::try_from_bytes_mut::<RecordData>(
        &mut raw_data[..RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX],
    ).map_err(|_| ProgramError::InvalidArgument)?;

    if !account_data.is_initialized() {
        return Err(ProgramError::UninitializedAccount);
    }
    check_authority(authority_info, &account_data.authority)?;

    let destination_starting_lamports = destination_info.lamports();
    let data_lamports = data_info.lamports();
    **data_info.lamports.borrow_mut() = 0;
    **destination_info.lamports.borrow_mut() = destination_starting_lamports
        .checked_add(data_lamports)
        .ok_or(RecordError::Overflow)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls - MITIGATED
No external CPI involved — lamport transfer is done directly via account manipulation. checked_add prevents overflow. Authority and initialization checks before any state modification.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction - kept authority check, initialization check, checked_add for lamport transfer (L129-131)

    let (expected_escrow_authority, bump_seed) = get_token_upgrade_authority_address_and_bump_seed(
        original_mint_info.key, new_mint_info.key, program_id);
    let bump_seed = [bump_seed];
    let authority_seeds = collect_token_upgrade_authority_signer_seeds(
        original_mint_info.key, new_mint_info.key, &bump_seed);

    if expected_escrow_authority != *new_transfer_authority_info.key {
        return Err(TokenUpgradeError::InvalidOwner.into());
    }

    transfer_new_tokens(new_token_program.clone(), new_escrow_info.clone(),
        new_mint_info.clone(), new_account_info.clone(), new_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        &authority_seeds, token_amount, decimals)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

fn process_exchange(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let (token_amount, decimals) = {
        let original_mint_data = original_mint_info.try_borrow_data()?;
        let original_mint = StateWithExtensions::<Mint>::unpack(&original_mint_data)?;
        let new_mint_data = new_mint_info.try_borrow_data()?;
        let new_mint = StateWithExtensions::<Mint>::unpack(&new_mint_data)?;

        let original_account_data = original_account_info.try_borrow_data()?;
        let original_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&original_account_data)?;
        let new_escrow_data = new_escrow_info.try_borrow_data()?;
        let new_escrow = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&new_escrow_data)?;

        let token_amount = original_account.base.amount;
        if new_escrow.base.amount < token_amount {
            return Err(ProgramError::InsufficientFunds);
        }
        if original_mint.base.decimals != new_mint.base.decimals {
            return Err(TokenUpgradeError::DecimalsMismatch.into());
        }
        (original_account.base.amount, original_mint.base.decimals)
    };
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE

RecordInstruction::Initialize => {
    let data_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let raw_data = &mut data_info.data.borrow_mut();
    if raw_data.len() < RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData);
    }

    let account_data = bytemuck::try_from_bytes_mut::<RecordData>(
        &mut raw_data[..RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX],
    ).map_err(|_| ProgramError::InvalidArgument)?;

    if account_data.is_initialized() {
        return Err(ProgramError::AccountAlreadyInitialized);
    }

    account_data.authority = *authority_info.key;
    account_data.version = RecordData::CURRENT_VERSION;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
RecordInstruction::Write { offset, data } => {
    let data_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    {
        let raw_data = &data_info.data.borrow();
        if raw_data.len() < RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX {
            return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData);
        }
        let account_data = bytemuck::try_from_bytes::<RecordData>(
            &raw_data[..RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX],
        ).map_err(|_| ProgramError::InvalidArgument)?;
        if !account_data.is_initialized() {
            return Err(ProgramError::UninitializedAccount);
        }
        check_authority(authority_info, &account_data.authority)?;
    }
    let start = RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX.saturating_add(offset as usize);
    let end = start.saturating_add(data.len());
    if end > data_info.data.borrow().len() {
        Err(ProgramError::AccountDataTooSmall)
    } else {
        data_info.data.borrow_mut()[start..end].copy_from_slice(data);
        Ok(())
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V4 - Input Validation - MITIGATED
Initialization check, authority check, saturating_add for offset calculation prevents overflow, bounds check (end > data_len) prevents buffer overflow write. Data written only within validated range.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction

Code:
fn process_tally(accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let feature_proposal_state =
        FeatureProposal::unpack_from_slice(&feature_proposal_info.data.borrow())?;

    match feature_proposal_state {
        FeatureProposal::Pending(acceptance_criteria) => {
            if clock.unix_timestamp >= acceptance_criteria.deadline {
                FeatureProposal::Expired
                    .pack_into_slice(&mut feature_proposal_info.data.borrow_mut());
                return Ok(());
            }

            let acceptance_token =
                spl_token::state::Account::unpack(&acceptance_token_info.data.borrow())?;
            if acceptance_token.amount < acceptance_criteria.tokens_required {
                return Ok(());
            }

            invoke_signed(
                &system_instruction::assign(feature_id_info.key, &feature::id()),
                &[feature_id_info.clone(), system_program_info.clone()],
                &[feature_id_signer_seeds],
            )?;
            FeatureProposal::Accepted { tokens_upon_acceptance: acceptance_token.amount }
                .pack_into_slice(&mut feature_proposal_info.data.borrow_mut());
        }
        _ => return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData),
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
Only Pending proposals can be tallied. Expired proposals gracefully transition to Expired state. Insufficient tokens return Ok (no revert, just no-op). Feature activation only after threshold met.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept state match (only Pending allowed), deadline check (L258-262), threshold check (L268-271), graceful non-error returns for insufficient tokens

fn process_tally(accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let acceptance_token =
        spl_token::state::Account::unpack(&acceptance_token_info.data.borrow())?;

    invoke_signed(
        &system_instruction::assign(feature_id_info.key, &feature::id()),
        &[feature_id_info.clone(), system_program_info.clone()],
        &[feature_id_signer_seeds],
    )?;

    FeatureProposal::Accepted { tokens_upon_acceptance: acceptance_token.amount }
        .pack_into_slice(&mut feature_proposal_info.data.borrow_mut());
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V10 - Denial of Service
CRITICAL
No state check allows tallying already-accepted or expired proposals. No deadline check allows premature acceptance. No threshold check allows acceptance with zero tokens. Feature activated without consensus.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze code for vulnerabilities according to OWASP Top 10 for Blockchain. Respond with the vulnerability status and details.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
RecordInstruction::Reallocate { data_length } => {
    let data_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    {
        let raw_data = &mut data_info.data.borrow_mut();
        let account_data = bytemuck::try_from_bytes_mut::<RecordData>(
            &mut raw_data[..RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX],
        ).map_err(|_| ProgramError::InvalidArgument)?;
        if !account_data.is_initialized() {
            return Err(ProgramError::UninitializedAccount);
        }
        check_authority(authority_info, &account_data.authority)?;
    }

    let needed_account_length = std::mem::size_of::<RecordData>()
        .checked_add(usize::try_from(data_length).map_err(|_| ProgramError::InvalidArgument)?)
        .unwrap();

    if data_info.data_len() >= needed_account_length {
        return Ok(());
    }
    data_info.resize(needed_account_length)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
Authority check prevents unauthorized reallocation. Initialization check. checked_add for size calculation. No-op if already large enough. usize::try_from validates data_length conversion. resize enforces system limits.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction

RecordInstruction::SetAuthority => {
    let data_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let new_authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    let raw_data = &mut data_info.data.borrow_mut();
    if raw_data.len() < RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData);
    }
    let account_data = bytemuck::try_from_bytes_mut::<RecordData>(
        &mut raw_data[..RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX],
    ).map_err(|_| ProgramError::InvalidArgument)?;

    if !account_data.is_initialized() {
        return Err(ProgramError::UninitializedAccount);
    }
    check_authority(authority_info, &account_data.authority)?;
    account_data.authority = *new_authority_info.key;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V1 - Access Control - MITIGATED
check_authority validates: (1) authority key matches stored authority, (2) authority is a signer. Initialization check prevents modifying uninitialized records. Only current authority can transfer control.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

fn process_exchange(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let (token_amount, decimals) = {
        let original_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&original_account_data)?;
        let new_escrow = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&new_escrow_data)?;
        if new_escrow.base.amount < token_amount {
            return Err(ProgramError::InsufficientFunds);
        }
        (original_account.base.amount, original_mint.base.decimals)
    };
    // Data borrows dropped here — safe for CPI

    // FIRST: Burn original tokens (destroys user's originals)
    burn_original_tokens(original_token_program.clone(), original_account_info.clone(),
        original_mint_info.clone(), original_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        account_info_iter.as_slice(), token_amount, decimals)?;

    // THEN: Transfer new tokens from escrow to user
    transfer_new_tokens(new_token_program.clone(), new_escrow_info.clone(),
        new_mint_info.clone(), new_account_info.clone(), new_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        &authority_seeds, token_amount, decimals)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Correct CEI pattern: data borrows dropped before CPIs, original tokens burned FIRST, then new tokens transferred. During transfer CPI, originals are already destroyed. Escrow balance pre-validated.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept data borrow/drop pattern (L108-140), burn-then-transfer CPI ordering (burn L143-151, transfer L153-162)

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze code for vulnerabilities according to OWASP Top 10 for Blockchain. Respond with the vulnerability status and details.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
fn process_exchange(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let original_account = StateWithExtensions::<Account>::unpack(&original_account_data)?;
    let token_amount = original_account.base.amount;
    let decimals = original_mint.base.decimals;

    // WRONG: Transfer new tokens FIRST
    transfer_new_tokens(new_token_program.clone(), new_escrow_info.clone(),
        new_mint_info.clone(), new_account_info.clone(), new_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        &authority_seeds, token_amount, decimals)?;

    // THEN burn — but user already has new tokens
    burn_original_tokens(original_token_program.clone(), original_account_info.clone(),
        original_mint_info.clone(), original_transfer_authority_info.clone(),
        account_info_iter.as_slice(), token_amount, decimals)?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks
CRITICAL
New tokens transferred before originals are burned. During the transfer CPI, an attacker can re-enter and exchange again with the same original tokens. The burn hasn't happened yet, so the originals still exist.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - reversed CPI order: transfer_new_tokens (originally L153) before burn_original_tokens (originally L143), removed escrow balance check, removed data borrow/drop pattern

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze code for vulnerabilities according to OWASP Top 10 for Blockchain. Respond with the vulnerability status and details.<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>

You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
RecordInstruction::Write { offset, data } => {
    let data_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let authority_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    check_authority(authority_info, &account_data.authority)?;

    let start = RecordData::WRITABLE_START_INDEX.saturating_add(offset as usize);
    let end = start.saturating_add(data.len());
    if end > data_info.data.borrow().len() {
        Err(ProgramError::AccountDataTooSmall)
    } else {
        data_info.data.borrow_mut()[start..end].copy_from_slice(data);
        Ok(())
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V8 - Integer Overflow - MITIGATED
saturating_add prevents overflow in offset and length calculations. If overflow would occur, values saturate to usize::MAX, which will always exceed data_len, triggering AccountDataTooSmall error.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept saturating_add for start (L76) and end (L77) calculations, bounds check (L78-79)
